summary.intent: Fix a series of bugs in the Gossamer VS Code extension: server port conflicts, missing database schema, checkpoint treeview not showing, multi-repo DB architecture, and add new features (checkpoint remote support, MIT license, UI improvements).
summary.outcome: Successfully fixed all reported bugs including EADDRINUSE orphan server handling, pushSchema silently discarding all SQL statements, per-repo DB client architecture replacing a single primary DB, gitlog maxBuffer overflow killing checkpoint git OID mappings, and added checkpoint remote (bare clone) support with auto-detection from .entire/settings.json.

repo: Each repo has its own SQLite DB at ~/.gossamer/repos/<name>.db — there is no primary/hierarchy among DBs, they are all peers.
pushSchema splits schema.sql on ';\n' but every chunk starts with '-- CreateTable' comments, so the old filter '!s.startsWith("--")' silently discarded all 56 CREATE TABLE statements.

The gitlog npm package defaults to nameStatus:true which appends --name-status to git log; on large repos (9000+ commits) this causes ERR_CHILD_PROCESS_STDIO_MAXBUFFER, silently caught, resulting in 0 git OID mappings and no branch history in the treeview.

Checkpoint commits live on entire/checkpoints/v1 branch with 'Checkpoint:' in subject, but feature branch commits have 'Entire-Checkpoint:' trailers — findCommitForCheckpoint searches feature branches for 'Entire-Checkpoint:' which is correct.
macOS com.apple.provenance extended attribute on DB files causes SQLite 'disk I/O error'; the fix is to evict cached Prisma client, delete the file, and retry.
The git worktree for entire/checkpoints/v1 must use --detach to avoid 'branch already used by worktree' errors when multiple repos share the same branch name.
Shadow branch refs are loose files under .git/refs/heads/entire/ — the fs.watch on .git/refs/ fires immediately on ref updates, enabling real-time indexing.

Entire.io checkpoint_remote config is in .entire/settings.json under strategy_options.checkpoint_remote.repo; the full git URL must be derived from the main repo's push remote to determine SSH vs HTTPS protocol.
The dbRegistry (Map<dbPath, PrismaClient>) pattern with allDbs()/dbForRepo()/resolveDb() helpers cleanly replaces the single 'db' variable across ~20 endpoints.
GET /api/repos/current was workspace-unaware — it only matched the server's startup repoDir, causing every other workspace to get null and re-register on every activation.

code.finding: pushSchema was only called for the primary DB at startup, not for all configured repo DBs — shadow/checkpoint indexers failed with 'table does not exist' for secondary repos.
catch block in saveGitOidMapping swallows ALL errors silently, masking the maxBuffer overflow from gitlog.
findCommitForCheckpoint uses gitlog with nameStatus defaulting to true — must pass nameStatus:false and execOptions:{maxBuffer: 100MB} to avoid overflow on large repos.
None of the webview API calls passed ?localPath= query param, so resolveDb() always probed all DBs sequentially instead of going directly to the right one.
EventItem rendered without user prop, so human turns always showed 'You' instead of the git username from session.gitUserName.
GET /api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints used bare 'db' (primary client) — returned [] for sessions in any other repo's DB.
spawnServer() guard only checked in-memory GossamerPanel.server static variable — orphaned server processes from previous VS Code sessions caused EADDRINUSE on reload.

workflow: When a SQLite DB shows 'schema ready' in logs but tables don't exist, check if the SQL file is being parsed correctly — comment-filtering on split chunks is a subtle failure mode.
Always test pushSchema against a real SQLite file with a Node script before trusting it works; Prisma $executeRawUnsafe is silent on empty statement arrays.
Use 'nameStatus: false' with the gitlog npm package for any large repo search — the default --name-status flag blows the maxBuffer on repos with many commits.
When introducing a Map-based registry to replace a singleton, update POST and DELETE handlers immediately to maintain registry consistency on dynamic add/remove.
macOS xattrs (com.apple.provenance) can corrupt SQLite files — always add retry-with-evict logic when creating DBs programmatically.
For git worktrees shared across repos, always use --detach to avoid branch locking conflicts.

friction: pushSchema fix required three iterations because the root cause (comment filter discarding all chunks) was masked by a second issue (macOS xattr disk I/O error on gossamer-test.db) — both had to be fixed independently.

The gitlog maxBuffer failure was completely silent — the catch in saveGitOidMapping consumed the error, making diagnosis require manual Node.js testing with process.execFile to observe the actual exception.
Entire CLI stopped writing shadow branch commits ~3 weeks before the session — all live-update debugging was blocked because the root data source wasn't generating new data.
The 'no primary DB' refactor touched ~20 endpoints across ~600 lines of server.ts, requiring careful read-before-edit of each section to avoid missing references.
resolveDb probe for GET /api/sessions/:id only checked shadowSession, missing sessions that exist only in checkpointSessionMetadata or logEvent — required a follow-up fix.
open_items: Entire CLI not writing new shadow branch commits for cosmos-graphql since ~3 weeks ago — needs investigation into whether the Entire background watcher process died or hooks are silently failing.
GET /api/sessions and checkpoint endpoints still only query a single repo's DB when no ?localPath= is provided and the session isn't found via probe — could be slow with many repos.
Gossamer has no fallback to show sessions that exist only on disk (.entire/metadata/) but have no shadow branch commits — orphaned sessions are invisible.
The checkpoint remote bare clone path uses a sanitized repo name from the URL — if two repos have the same name (different orgs), they'd collide in ~/.gossamer/checkpoint-repos/.

Live session updates from cosmos-graphql still unverified end-to-end because Entire CLI isn't writing new data — the indexing chain was confirmed working via simulation but not with real Entire output.
The 'Claude Code' agent label in SessionDetail is already dynamic (uses session.agent), but it's unclear if all agent types are correctly set during indexing.
